common_symptoms: unable to retract caliper for pad change
rear brake drag after pad replacement
physical_solution: Activate EPB service mode to retract rear caliper pistons before pad/rotor replacement. Must release brake fully before piston retract.

technical_solution.elm327_feasibility: Partial — accessible on most OEMs; Stellantis 2018+ SGW may block; BMW ZGW may restrict on G-series

procedure_steps.description: Connect OBD2 adapter. Key ON, engine OFF. Ensure vehicle is on level surface.
Start EPB service routine: $31 01 [OEM_ROUTINE_ID]. Routine IDs vary by OEM — see oem_notes.
Poll routine result: $31 03 [OEM_ROUTINE_ID]. Confirm positive response.
Apply brake firmly 5 times to re-seat pads. Verify parking brake function.

oem_notes.Toyota: Routine IDs documented. Broadly accessible via ELM327.
oem_notes.Honda: $31 routine via F-CAN (pins 6/14). DID varies by model year.
oem_notes.Nissan: $31 or $2F depending on platform. CONSULT session sequence may be needed.
oem_notes.Ford: $31 on HS-CAN (pins 6/14). Older models more accessible; session/security on 2019+.
oem_notes.GM: $31 or $2F. HS-CAN only — SW-CAN body bus not needed for EPB on most platforms.
oem_notes.BMW: $31 via ZGW. F-series accessible; G-series (2018+) ZGW may restrict.
oem_notes.VAG: $31 via J533. Well-documented in VCDS community. SFD on 2019+ may block.
oem_notes.Stellantis_2018plus: SGW blocks — surface to mechanic that dealer tool may be required.
